creator: hakan@siktas.com
text:
Hi,

I am not sure if this is a gitlab-ce bug or if I just messed up my setup.
Initially, the upgrade to www/gitlab-ce 11.5.0 went smoothly but a few days later I got it in a state where it would refuse to stop/restart the service because it could not regenerate its Gemfile.lock anymore. Afair, this was the result of some updated rubygem ports that I installed in the meantime.
After rebooting the server, GitLab does not work anymore. I did reinstall the port several times and also did the update to 11.5.1 but somehow it got worse...

When I try to run the database migration I get:

su -l git -c "cd /usr/local/www/gitlab-ce && rake db:migrate RAILS_ENV=production"
/usr/local/www/gitlab-ce/lib/api/events.rb:21: warning: already initialized constant API::Events::RedactedEvent
/usr/local/www/gitlab-ce/lib/api/events.rb:21: warning: previous definition of RedactedEvent was here
rake aborted!
NoMethodError: undefined method `demodulize' for nil:NilClass

Tasks: TOP => db:migrate => environment
(See full trace by running task with --trace)

Actually, every rake task fails with the same error. Before upgrading to 11.5.1 I tried to reinstall 11.5.0 and `rake gettext:compile RAILS_ENV=production` and `rake yarn:install gitlab:assets:clean gitlab:assets:compile RAILS_ENV=production NODE_ENV=production` showed the same error.

Does someone have an idea? I am not a ruby developer but even `rake -T` and `rake -A` are also failing with the same error:

/usr/local/www/gitlab-ce # rake -T
rake aborted!
LoadError: cannot load such file -- haml_lint/rake_task
/usr/local/www/gitlab-ce/lib/tasks/haml-lint.rake:2:in `<top (required)>'
/usr/local/www/gitlab-ce/Rakefile:10:in `<top (required)>'
(See full trace by running task with --trace)
